title: myoporum sandwicense subsp. wilderi
intro: Myoporum sandwicense subsp. wilderi is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Myoporaceae, native to Hawaii, and is also known as Wilderi's Myoporum. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Myoporum sandwicense subsp. wilderi include its ability to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and salves, which can be applied topically or taken orally to reap its medicinal benefits.
